source_title: A DIY, Project-based Approach to Teaching Data Journalism
author: Caroline Graham

extracts.extract: DJ has a tendency to be more exploratory than other kinds of investigative reporting, which usually begin with a hunch or tip-off and have a much clearer investigative trajectory. It can be a challenge to find data sets that will have currency at the semester's conclusion, will deliver a range of strong news leads and will sustain student engagement. In practice, this usually means choosing an area of research where the public interest value is high enough that if students begin with a broad, exploratory question their findings will be newsworthy, whatever the result.
extracts.notes: Contrasts DJ exploratory orientation with traditional tip-off investigative work
extracts.relevance_rationale: DJ as inherently exploratory, requiring high-public-interest topics where any finding is newsworthy, is editorial judgment that endures

extracts.extract: One of the most challenging elements of a data-driven investigation is conceptualizing a research strategy that will provide meaningful and reliable results and conclusions. This design process is more closely aligned with academic research strategies than journalistic practice.
extracts.notes: Positions DJ research design as closer to academic than journalistic traditions
extracts.relevance_rationale: Conceptualizing a research strategy for meaningful and reliable results is a design skill closer to academic rigor than journalism tradition and endures

extracts.extract: Nguyen and Lugo-Ocando suggest that 'permanent determination to question data and a basic level of statistical reasoning' is a necessary starting point, as well as the ability to use logical, valid reasoning and journalistic scepticism to (a) find and acquire data, (b) explore and evaluate their real meaning, (c) investigate non-numerical factors shaping them and (d) report them in a balanced, fair, accurate, accessible and engaging manner.
extracts.notes: Four capabilities framework from Nguyen and Lugo-Ocando 2016
extracts.relevance_rationale: Permanent determination to question data and statistical reasoning as foundations for finding, evaluating, and reporting data endure as core competencies

extracts.extract: In a news conference format, the class considers some of the best ways to measure and evidence a phenomenon, and examines the potential gaps and weaknesses in the data. They begin to debate and ask questions: Does giving a lot of speeches make someone a better politician or just a louder one? Are some speeches more valid than others? Can we find out what each MP is talking about? What work might a politician do that isn't captured in the data?
extracts.notes: News conference format used for collaborative methodology design
extracts.relevance_rationale: News conference format for collaborative methodology design -- debating measurement validity and data gaps -- is active learning that endures

extracts.extract: The course would focus on DJ as the foundational research method. The data and analysis could guarantee strong, exclusive stories which students could then break into research areas and supplement with documents, interviews, analysis, visualization and storytelling, later in the process.
extracts.chapter: Towards a Project-based Approach: A Case Study
extracts.notes: Data-first approach: collect data first, then supplement with interviews and documents
extracts.relevance_rationale: Data-first workflow producing exclusive stories supplemented by interviews and documents is a research strategy that endures

extracts.extract: The most successful projects over the last six years have involved a mixture of hand-collected/coded data and open source or scraped data. Despite its potential to be monotonous, manual data coding activities are often identified by students as a highlight of the course and seeing the data at a micro-level helps the students understand more complex spreadsheets. Students approach data as an exercise in translation, and the act of translating information to data is perhaps more accessible than the reverse.
extracts.notes: Counter-intuitive finding: manual coding valued despite available automated tools
extracts.relevance_rationale: Manual data coding builds micro-level understanding and produces exclusive data; the pedagogical value of translation from information to data endures

extracts.extract: Even a simple, one-hour data collection exercise, if tied to the project outcomes, helps to explain the nature of data and to familiarize students with how it can be structured. As a bonus, hand-coded data is also exclusive data.
extracts.relevance_rationale: Short hands-on data collection exercises tied to project outcomes build structural understanding of data and produce exclusive material

extracts.extract: The cohort naturally treats the yet-to-be-collected data as they would any other source: they ask questions of it; consider its agendas, weaknesses and blind spots; are critical of it; examine its biases; contextualize it and cross-check it against other sources.
extracts.notes: Treats data with same critical scrutiny as human sources
extracts.relevance_rationale: Treating data with the same critical scrutiny as human sources -- questioning agendas, biases, blind spots -- is journalistic skepticism that endures

extracts.extract: This kind of critical engagement with the data takes place throughout the project: during the methodology design, data collection, data analysis, planning and researching the stories, visualizing the data and reporting findings.
extracts.notes: Verification is continuous across all pipeline stages, not a discrete step
extracts.relevance_rationale: Continuous critical engagement throughout all pipeline stages rather than verification as a discrete step is a principle that endures

extracts.extract: Getting each student to choose a free, online tool and visualize an element of their research is a worthwhile exercise, not just because it exposes students to some techniques and frameworks for visualizations but also because it intensifies students' connection to the data. The visualization process often prompts students to ask deeper questions about the data, and come to different conclusions about their results.
extracts.notes: Visualization as analytical tool, not just presentation
extracts.relevance_rationale: Visualization as an analytical tool that deepens connection to data and prompts new questions endures beyond its presentation function

extracts.extract: Complex or technical analysis (for example, correlations) can still be achieved through enlisting an expert with access to specialist software; fortunately, a university is full of such experts. In this case, the students must still develop the questions that drive the specialist data analysis, even if they do not conduct the analysis themselves, to ensure they are active participants and collaborators in this facet of the process.
extracts.notes: Journalists drive analytical questions even when specialists do the computation
extracts.relevance_rationale: LLMs now serve as the specialist collaborator for complex analysis, reducing dependence on university experts though question-formulation skill endures

extracts.extract: The major project is presented to students as an 'investigation', not as a 'data-driven investigation'. In the early weeks, all discussion is framed in the context of their topic. Students naturally self-identify a number of data-driven strategies for their investigation, well before anyone has mentioned statistics, maths or data. The project-driven approach allows the lecturer to normalize DJ as one of a range of journalistic tools a reporter has access to; the methodology does not define the students' investigation, the research questions do.
extracts.notes: Guerrilla approach: frame around questions not methodology to overcome resistance
extracts.relevance_rationale: Framing DJ as investigation not data-driven investigation to overcome dataphobia is a pedagogical strategy that remains effective

extracts.extract: One data mining mission can provide multiple leads for stories. If you think there are no stories out there and the world seems a bit boring: dig, search, compile a series of stats and it can lead you to some ammunition for a killer story.
extracts.chapter: The Benefits and Challenges of the Project-based Approach
extracts.notes: Student testimony; single dataset yields multiple story angles
extracts.relevance_rationale: One data mining exercise yielding multiple story leads is a discovery principle that endures and is amplified by AI-assisted exploration

extracts.extract: The most valuable investigative tool I have garnered from this exercise is the skill of being able to read data, in the sense that by changing the way pieces of information sit next to each other, you can have an entirely new outcome.
extracts.chapter: The Benefits and Challenges of the Project-based Approach
extracts.notes: Student testimony; rearranging data reveals new patterns
extracts.relevance_rationale: Reading data by juxtaposing information in new ways to reveal patterns is an analytical literacy skill that endures

extracts.extract: The emphasis on data logic, literacy, scepticism and research strategy is an approach that is likely to age well. While DJ technologies and skills are changing rapidly, the ability to problem-solve and collaborate through investigative roadblocks to find innovative solutions will retain its value.
extracts.chapter: The Benefits and Challenges of the Project-based Approach
extracts.notes: Principles over tools: critical thinking outlasts specific technologies
extracts.relevance_rationale: Data logic, literacy, skepticism, and problem-solving are explicitly designed to age well as transferable skills outlasting specific technologies

extracts.extract: The early weeks of the semester are used to conceptualize and define the scope of the project. Students then collect and analyse data, contextualize it with interviews and other research, and write and visualize their findings. Although the project is collaborative, the students identify sub-topics and niches to specialize in. They share information and collaborate with students working on the same sub-topic, and must manage overlap between their research and stories.
extracts.notes: End-to-end pipeline: scope -> collect -> analyse -> contextualize -> write/visualize
extracts.relevance_rationale: Collaborative project workflow from scoping through specialization and overlap management is project management that endures

extracts.extract: Visualizing the data was the first time I really understood the story it was telling. It made me really think about each line on the spreadsheet and what it really means.
extracts.notes: Student testimony; visualization as moment of analytical breakthrough
extracts.relevance_rationale: Visualization as the moment of analytical breakthrough and deeper data understanding is a learning insight that endures

extracts.extract: The assessment is broken into micro-assessments, including data collection, research plan, story draft, infographic or visualization, research journal, academic reflection and final investigative package. Wherever possible, the lecturer aims to work with industry to offer students a publication opportunity at the end of the project.
extracts.notes: Structured decomposition of a large investigation into discrete deliverables
extracts.relevance_rationale: Micro-assessments decomposing investigations into discrete deliverables with publication opportunities is sound pedagogy that endures
